name: openssh-client
title: openssh-client
summary: secure shell (SSH) client, for secure access to remote machines
description:
secure shell (SSH) client, for secure access to remote machines
This is the portable version of OpenSSH, a free implementation of
the Secure Shell protocol as specified by the IETF secsh working
group.
.
Ssh (Secure Shell) is a program for logging into a remote machine
and for executing commands on a remote machine.
It provides secure encrypted communications between two untrusted
hosts over an insecure network. X11 connections and arbitrary TCP/IP
ports can also be forwarded over the secure channel.
It can be used to provide applications with a secure communication
channel.
.
This package provides the ssh, scp and sftp clients, the ssh-agent
and ssh-add programs to make public key authentication more convenient,
and the ssh-keygen, ssh-keyscan, ssh-copy-id and ssh-argv0 utilities.
.
In some countries it may be illegal to use any encryption at all
without a special permit.
.
ssh replaces the insecure rsh, rcp and rlogin programs, which are
obsolete for most purposes.

arguments.description: Allows forwarding of ssh-agent(1) to the remote system. The default is not to forward
arguments.raw: -A  Allows forwarding of ssh-agent(1) to the remote system. The default is not to forward

arguments.description: Selects batch mode (prevents asking for passwords or passphrases).
arguments.raw: -B  Selects batch mode (prevents asking for passwords or passphrases).

arguments.description: Compression enable. Passes the -C flag to ssh(1) to enable compression.
arguments.raw: -C  Compression enable. Passes the -C flag to ssh(1) to enable compression.

arguments.description: the legacy SCP protocol for file transfers instead of the SFTP protocol. Forcing
arguments.raw: -O  the legacy SCP protocol for file transfers instead of the SFTP protocol. Forcing

arguments.description: Preserves modification times, access times, and file mode bits from the source file.
arguments.raw: -p  Preserves modification times, access times, and file mode bits from the source file.

arguments.description: Quiet mode: disables the progress meter as well as warning and diagnostic messages
arguments.raw: -q  Quiet mode: disables the progress meter as well as warning and diagnostic messages

arguments.description: between two remote hosts are transferred through the local host by default.
arguments.raw: -R  between two remote hosts are transferred through the local host by default.

arguments.description: Recursively copy entire directories. Note that scp follows symbolic links encountered
arguments.raw: -r  Recursively copy entire directories. Note that scp follows symbolic links encountered

arguments.description: Disable strict filename checking. By default when copying files from a remote host to
arguments.raw: -T  Disable strict filename checking. By default when copying files from a remote host to

arguments.description: Verbose mode. Causes scp and ssh(1) to print debugging messages about their progress.
arguments.raw: -v  Verbose mode. Causes scp and ssh(1) to print debugging messages about their progress.

commands.name: ssh-add
commands.description: adds private key identities to the OpenSSH authentication agent

commands.name: ssh-argv0
commands.description: replaces the old ssh command-name as hostname handling

commands.name: ssh-copy-id
commands.description: use locally available keys to authorise logins on a remote machine

commands.name: ssh-keyscan
commands.description: gather SSH public keys from servers

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use scp because secure shell (SSH) client, for secure access to remote machines.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use scp as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `scp [-346ABCOpqRrsTv] [-c cipher] [-D sftp_server_path] [-F ssh_config] [-i identity_file]` as documented upstream for scp.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair scp with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `scp --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
